topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on specific chemicals and electrical transformers.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on Methyl (E)-methoxyimino-2(2-o-tolyloxymethyl) phenyl) acetate (kresoxim methyl).
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to revise the heading of a specified chemical to provide for the temporary suspension of duty on mixtures of 2-tert-amyl 9,10-anthraquinone and 2-iso-amyl-9,10-anthraquinone.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain electrical transformers.

topic.name: Support for Women's Entrepreneurship
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at promoting and supporting women's entrepreneurship through various programs and initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Women's Business Ownership Act of 1988 to direct the National Women's Business Council to evaluate problems hindering the success of women entrepreneurs.
Revises matching funds requirements for women's business center projects.
Document 2: Direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make publicly available a user-friendly database of information relating to lenders making loans.
Prohibits the Administrator from requiring lenders to submit additional information or to otherwise increase burdens on lenders.

topic.name: Privacy and Surveillance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to privacy rights, data protection, and surveillance practices in the context of electronic communications and educational records.

Document 1: Electronic Communications Privacy Act Amendments Act of 2011
add geolocation information services to the category of electronic and remote computing service providers required to disclose customer or subscriber information
Document 2: Uninterrupted Scholars Act (USA)

Amends provisions of the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act of 1974 that prohibit the Department of Education from funding educational agencies or institutions that release student educational records
Document 3: Preserving Freedom from Unwarranted Surveillance Act of 2012
Prohibits a person or entity acting under the authority of the United States from using a drone to gather evidence or other information pertaining to criminal conduct

topic.name: Education Funding and Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to funding and programs aimed at improving educational access and quality for children and families.

Document 1: Amends the 21st Century Community Learning Centers program
Requires the Centers to provide activities that are targeted to their academic needs
Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
Establish a Prekindergarten Incentive Fund from which matching grants shall be awarded to states
Document 3: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act
Requires local educational agencies to work with public and private state and local entities to provide children and families with services

topic.name: Tax Deductions and Credits
topic.description: Legislation focused on amending the Internal Revenue Code to enhance various tax deductions and credits for education, health, and caregiving expenses.

Document 1: increase the modified adjusted gross income threshold for reducing the allowable amount of the tax deduction for interest on education loans
elimination of the 60-month limitation on the tax deduction for interest on education loans
Document 2: allow a medical care tax deduction for up to $1,000 of qualified sports and fitness expenses
Defines 'qualified sports and fitness expenses' as amounts paid for fitness center memberships, physical exercise programs, and exercise equipment.
Document 3: increase from $15,000 to $75,000 the taxpayer adjusted gross income amount at which the tax credit for household and dependent care expenses is phased down
allow such credit for a physically or mentally incapacitated parent of the taxpayer who is a dependent of such taxpayer

topic.name: Environmental Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at environmental conservation and management of water resources in various regions of the United States.

Document 1: Upper Mississippi Conservation and River Protection Act of 2012
manage the threat of Asian carp traveling up the Mississippi River
Document 2: San Francisco Bay Improvement Act of 2010
implementation of the comprehensive conservation and management plan for the San Francisco Bay
Document 3: Upper Connecticut River Partnership Act
provide grants and technical assistance to the governments of New Hampshire and Vermont for conservation projects

topic.name: Tax Credits for Renewable Energy
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts focused on providing tax credits and incentives for renewable energy projects and technologies.

Document 1: Fair Allocation of Internal Revenue Credit for Renewable Electricity Distribution by Indian Tribes Act of 2009
allow an Indian tribe... to assign to any other person... any portion of the production from the facility
Document 2: Global Forest Restoration Investment Tax Credit Act
allow a business-related tax credit for investment in a carbon sequestration project
Document 3: Supercritical Advanced Coal Project Incentive Act of 2012
expand the tax credit for investment in qualifying advanced coal projects

topic.name: Domestic Violence and Public Safety Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving legal support for victims of domestic violence, addressing missing persons cases, and enhancing penalties for sex offenders.

Document 1: National Domestic Violence Volunteer Attorney Network Act
requires the Attorney General to award grants to expand services for domestic violence victims.
Document 2: Billy's Law or the Help Find the Missing Act
establishes public databases to assist in identifying missing persons.
Document 3: No Parole for Sex Offenders Act
requires states to prohibit parole for individuals convicted of crimes against minors.

topic.name: Government Authority and Refugee Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses the government's powers related to law enforcement, veteran services, and refugee resettlement policies.

Document 1: Allows the Secretary of Defense to authorize a Department of Defense (DOD) law enforcement officer to: (1) execute and serve a warrant or other process; (2) make an arrest without a warrant for any offense committed in the presence of the officer, or for any felony if the officer has probable cause to believe that the person arrested has committed or is committing the felony; and (3) carry firearms.
the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) or the Secretary of the military department concerned to accept voluntary services performed by a veteran or veterans organization at a national cemetery
Document 2: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to prohibit the resettlement of refugees in a state whose governor has taken any action formally disapproving the resettlement of refugees, or of certain refugees, in that state during that fiscal year.

topic.name: Labor Rights and Protections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing labor rights, workplace safety, and protections for workers in various sectors.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to revise requirements for determination by the National Labor Relation Board (NLRB) of an appropriate bargaining unit before an election of collective bargaining representation.
Prohibits exclusion of employees from the unit unless the group's interest are sufficiently distinct from those of other employees to warrant the establishment of a separate unit.
Document 2: Waives a state's sovereign immunity with respect to the enforcement of uniformed services members' employment or reemployment rights or benefits under the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act of 1994 (USERRA).
Provides increased liquidated damages, and authorizes punitive damages, against state or private employer violations of USERRA.
Document 3: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 (OSHA) to expand its coverage to federal, state, and local government employees.
Sets forth increased protections for whistle blowers under OSHA.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring fairness and safety for consumers in various markets, including telecommunications and childcare products.

Document 1: Requires the Department of Commerce to implement and administer a program to obtain coupons...
Provides for a website and telephone hotline to provide relevant information to assist consumers...
Document 2: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to promulgate regulations...
Prohibits construing this Act to preempt related state or local laws.
Document 3: Bans as a hazardous product any drop-side crib that is sold, offered for sale...
Defines 'drop-side crib' as any infant crib that has at least one side that moves up and down...

topic.name: Land Transfer and Management Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the transfer, conveyance, and management of federal lands for economic development and conservation purposes.

Document 1: Transfers certain land in Riverside and San Diego counties, California, from the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to the United States to be held in trust for the Pechanga Band of Luiseno Mission Indians.

Prohibits such transfer from including the 12.82 acres of lands more or less, including the facilities, improvements, and appurtenances associated with the existing 230kV transmission line in San Diego County.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service, and the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Director of the Bureau of Land Management (BLM), to convey specified parcels of federal land to certain counties and cities in central Idaho.
Designates specified parcels of federal land in central Idaho as wilderness areas to be managed as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System (NWPS).
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey, without consideration, to the city of Anchorage, Alaska, the reversionary interests of the United States in specified parcels of non-federal land.
Requires the city to pay all costs associated with such conveyance.

topic.name: U.S. Intelligence and Records Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, preservation, and oversight of electronic records and intelligence activities within U.S. federal agencies.

Document 1: Requires the Archivist of the United States to promulgate regulations governing federal agency preservation of electronic messages that are federal records
establish standards for the management of presidential records during a President's term of office
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 to add a new title concerning additional procedures for acquiring the communications of certain persons outside the United States

prohibiting an acquisition intentionally targeting a person reasonably believed to be outside the United States in order to acquire the communications of a specific person reasonably believed to be located in the United States
Document 3: authorizes FY2016 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
the DNI must establish guidelines to govern the treatment under such authorized personnel levels of employment or assignment

topic.name: Water Resource Management Legislation
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at improving water resource management and infrastructure in the United States.

Document 1: directs the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to submit a report including...
carry out at least 15 pilot projects...
Document 2: requires the Secretary of the Army to take actions to ensure...
the installation of structures in the North Shore Channel...
Document 3: requires the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to enter cooperative agreements...
the Corps of Engineers must use its existing channel depths and dimensions authority...

topic.name: Communication and Spectrum Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies related to communication technologies, spectrum management, and accessibility in telecommunications.

Document 1: Reforming Airwaves by Developing Incentives and Opportunistic Sharing Act or the RADIOS Act - Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require the Federal Communications Commission (FCC)...
Directs the FCC and NTIA to conduct a study of occupancy on the electromagnetic spectrum.
Document 2: Twenty-First Century Communications and Video Accessibility Act of 2010 - Title I: Communications Access - Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to require that customer premises equipment provide internal means for effective use with hearing aids...

topic.name: Legislative Amendments on Safety and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative amendments aimed at enhancing safety and regulatory measures across different sectors, including transportation and criminal justice.

Document 1: Amends the Death on the High Seas Act to make provisions concerning commercial aviation accidents also applicable to maritime accidents.
Document 2: Allows railroad employees to remain or go on duty for a period in excess of the limitations established under hours-of-service requirements to the extent necessary to clear a blockage of vehicular traffic at a grade crossing.
Document 3: Directs the Attorney General to make grants to assist states with costs associated with the implementation of minimum or enhanced DNA collection processes.

topic.name: Social Security Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Social Security Act regarding retirement age, Medicare prescription drug costs, and benefits for divorced spouses.

Document 1: increases the age for retirement to 70
early retirement age to 65 as of January 1, 2069
Document 2: limit to $10, adjusted for inflation, the monthly amount of prescription drug cost-sharing
for full-benefit dual eligible individuals
Document 3: eliminate the two-year waiting period following a divorce
to become entitled to a spouse's OASDI benefits

topic.name: Education and Community Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving education and community support through various programs and initiatives.

Document 1: establish a pilot program awarding grants to 10 state or local educational agencies
integrate financial literacy programs into elementary and secondary school curricula
Document 2: establish the Haiti Professional Exchange Program
provide technical assistance to help Haiti improve in areas vital to its growth and development
Document 3: conduct a pilot program under which the Secretary will give up to five participating states the option of receiving a grant
purchase fresh fruits and vegetables for distribution to schools

topic.name: Small Business Support Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing support and funding for small businesses through various programs and initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the authority of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to use certain SBA programs
Directs the SBA to report annually to specified congressional committees regarding all entrepreneurial development activities
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish the Champlain Quadricentennial Commemoration Commission
Authorizes the Champlain Commission to make grants not to exceed $20,000 to communities, nonprofit organizations, and commemorative commissions
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act to reauthorize through FY2019 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
Increases by 0.1% per year the percentage of participating federal agencies' extramural research budget to be set aside for SBIR program activities

topic.name: Offshore Energy Development
topic.description: Legislation and regulations governing the exploration, development, and leasing of oil and gas resources in offshore areas and federal lands.

Document 1: approved the Draft Proposed Outer Continental Shelf Oil and Gas Leasing Program 2010-2015
implement a competitive leasing program for the exploration, development, and production of the oil and gas resources on the Coastal Plain of Alaska
Document 2: Amends the Submerged Lands Act regarding delineation of lateral offshore state boundaries
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to include, in each five-year OCS leasing program, lease sales that propose to offer to lease at least 75% of the available unleased acreage
Document 3: Authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain the pipeline
deems the Final Supplemental Environmental Impact Statement regarding the pipeline to fully satisfy the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969

topic.name: Regulation of Food and Animal Safety
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation of genetically engineered food, the use of performance-enhancing drugs in horse racing, and the protection of nonhuman primates in commerce.

Document 1: prohibit the sale of food that has been genetically engineered
labeling or advertising foods containing genetically engineered material as 'natural' is prohibited
Document 2: prohibit entering a horse in a race that is subject to an interstate off-track wager if the person knows the horse is under the influence of a performance-enhancing drug
requires that an accredited third party conformity assessment body test the first-place horse
Document 3: makes it unlawful for a person to sell or purchase a live animal of any prohibited wildlife species
civil and criminal penalties for violations of the requirements of this Act

topic.name: Immigration and National Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures related to immigration policies and national security concerns.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act, in the case of an alien subject to removal, deportation, or exclusion and who is the parent of a U.S. citizen child
authorize an immigration judge to decline to order such removal if the judge determines that action to be against the child's best interests.
Document 2: no alien who is a refugee because of the conflict in Syria may be admitted to the United States as a refugee
no funds may be expended to process refugee applications for covered aliens.
Document 3: prohibits assistance under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 and the Arms Export Control Act for five years to a foreign country
if the country received an individual who was released or transferred from United States Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.

topic.name: Education Funding and Equity
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving financial aid and support for educators and students in low-income educational settings.

Document 1: increases the amount of loan forgiveness available under the Federal Family Education Loan (FFEL) and Direct Loan (DL) programs to highly qualified teachers
Requires those low income schools to be schools at which more than 30% of the students are impoverished
Document 2: amends the Improving America's Schools Act of 1994 to extend through FY2022 the antitrust exemption that allows institutions of higher education that admit all students on a need-blind basis to enter or attempt to enter into agreements regarding need-based financial aid
Document 3: requires information on the annual state report cards on student achievement to be disaggregated by major racial groups
disaggregation and cross-tabulation shall not be required when the number of students in a subgroup is insufficient to yield statistically reliable information

topic.name: Environmental Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at environmental conservation and resource management.

Document 1: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to make the Act's provisions regarding the determination of endangered or threatened species inapplicable to specific salamander species.
Salamander Community Conservation Act
Document 2: Amends the Coastal Barrier Resources Act to remove areas from the Coastal Barrier Resources System.
Removal of specific units in Florida from conservation designation.

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Energy Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to environmental protection, energy production, and regulatory frameworks governing fuel and energy resources.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to apply the authority of the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to waive prohibitions on the introduction into commerce of certain fuels or fuel additives.
Require the Administrator to grant or deny a waiver application, after public notice and comment, within 270 days.
Document 2: Directs the President to promulgate regulations to ensure that aviation fuel, motor vehicle fuel, home heating oil, and boiler fuel sold or introduced into commerce contains the applicable volume of clean coal-derived fuel.
Allows a small refinery to petition the President for an exemption at any time for the reason of disproportionate economic hardship.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Power Act to direct the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to give equal consideration to private landownership when deciding whether to issue a hydropower project license.
Requires a hydropower project licensee to consider private landownership as a means to encourage and facilitate private investment.

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Reform
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation, reform, and enforcement of immigration laws in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to issue final regulations to eliminate or reduce fraud in the special immigrant non-minister religious worker program
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to extend the special immigrant program for non-minister religious workers until January 1, 2010
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish an annual cap on H-1B visas
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to authorize the accompanying spouse of an H-1B alien to work in the United States
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to subject a person to fine, imprisonment, or both, who willfully and knowingly executes a scheme in connection with any federal immigration law-related matter to defraud a person
Directs the Attorney General and the Secretary of Homeland Security to educate immigrant communities about who may provide legal services and representation in immigration proceedings

topic.name: Social Security and Disability Benefits
topic.description: Legislation focused on reforming social security, disability benefits, and related employment protections for veterans and workers.

Document 1: amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act
new benefit formula for retired and disabled worker beneficiaries
Document 2: amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993
provide eligibility for specified medical/hospital care for an employee who has a Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) disability rating
Document 3: amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008
requires a further additional Tier-5 period for deposits to an individual's EUCA

topic.name: Peace and Conflict Resolution Policies
topic.description: The documents collectively address legislative measures aimed at promoting peace, conflict resolution, and addressing international relations through various frameworks.

Document 1: Establishes a Department of Peace, which shall be headed by a Secretary of Peace
development of policies that promote national and international conflict prevention, nonviolent intervention
Document 2: Authorizes the use of alternative methodologies in determining whether a subsidy is countervailable with respect to the People's Republic of China
Prohibits the administering authority from considering requests for market economy treatment at the individual business enterprise level

topic.name: Veterans' Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving health care access and support for military personnel and veterans.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to allow certain licensed health-care professionals to provide care to members of the Armed Forces at any location
Requires a report from the Secretary to Congress on plans to develop and expand programs to use new Internet and communication technologies, including telemedicine.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Defense to carry out programs under which commissioned officers of the nurse corps serve as full-time faculty of accredited schools of nursing
Requires the Secretary to assess each program to determine if it should be continued.
Document 3: Requires the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to reconsider and make a new determination regarding each claim for disability compensation
The VA shall report to Congress every 90 days on reconsidered claims that were denied.

topic.name: Mental Health and Substance Abuse Reform
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at improving mental health services, substance abuse treatment, and healthcare integration for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: amends the Public Health Service Act to revise SAMHSA authorities
HHS must maintain a Minority Fellowship Program for mental and substance use disorder treatment professionals
Document 2: extends the authority to restrict enrollment for specialized Medicare Advantage plans for special needs individuals
align Medicare and Medicaid policies and procedures for dual eligibles
Document 3: establishes programs regarding children's nutrition and physical activity
promoting equal opportunities for students with disabilities

topic.name: Disaster Risk Management and Recovery
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and programs aimed at assessing and improving disaster risk management, recovery efforts, and the financial stability of insurance markets following catastrophic events.

Document 1: Establishes the bipartisan Commission on Catastrophic Disaster Risk and Insurance to assess the condition of the property and casualty insurance and reinsurance markets in the aftermath of Hurricane Katrina, Rita, and Wilma in 2005.
recommend and report legislative and regulatory changes to improve the domestic and international financial health and competitiveness of such markets.
Document 2: Funds appropriated for the Missouri River Fish and Wildlife Recovery shall be available only for the reconstruction of flood control structures until the structures are returned to the elevation and design that predates the 2011 Missouri River flood event.

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Oversight
topic.description: Documents discuss various federal regulations and oversight mechanisms related to housing, payments, and online gambling.

Document 1: Amends the National Housing Act to direct the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to establish and collect additional annual premium payments.
Revises conditions for the Secretary's exercise of authority to require indemnification for an insurance loss claim.
Document 2: Amends the Improper Payments Elimination and Recovery Improvement Act to require the Commissioner of the Social Security Administration to establish and maintain the National Deaths Registry.
Directs the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to identify each agency that operates or maintains a database relating to federal beneficiaries.

topic.name: Biofuels and Carbon Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the development and implementation of biofuels and carbon capture technologies to enhance energy sustainability and reduce emissions.

Document 1: establish a technology transfer center to disseminate information on research, development, and commercial application of technologies related to specified biofuels
requires the Secretary to report to Congress on challenges inherent in increasing to 2.5% the biodiesel proportion of diesel fuel sold in the United States.
Document 2: establish a cooperative industry-government research and development program to demonstrate innovative technologies to capture, prevent, or store carbon dioxide emissions
authorize DOE to make loan guarantees to construct or retrofit coal- or petroleum coke-fired industrial facilities or electric generation facilities to utilize CCS technology.
Document 3: offer to enter into agreements to make capitalization grants to eligible states for the repair or replacement of natural gas distribution pipelines

prohibits the use of funds from loans or loan guarantees made by a state to repair or replace natural gas distribution pipelines unless all of the iron, steel, plastic, and manufactured goods used in the repair or replacement are produced in the United States.

topic.name: Pharmaceutical Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative measures and amendments related to the regulation, approval, and safety monitoring of pharmaceuticals and medical products.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to include postmarket safety activities
developing and using improved adverse event data collection systems
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act with respect to labeling in the shipment or delivery of an in vitro diagnostic product
may not be deemed misbranded on the basis that its manufacturer or distributor sells the product to an end user
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to revise the definition of 'first applicant' for purposes of the 180-day exclusivity period
makes applicants for a generic drug eligible for the exclusivity period only if they have not entered into a disqualifying agreement

topic.name: Healthcare Regulation and Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring transparency, integrity, and access within the healthcare system.

Document 1: Prohibits any deceptive or misleading statement in healthcare services advertising
Requires disclosure of applicable licenses for healthcare services
Document 2: Requires claims dispute resolution procedures for long-term care insurance
Ensures independent third-party review in claims disputes
Document 3: Prohibits group health plans from denying participation in cancer clinical trials
Ensures coverage of routine patient costs in clinical trials

topic.name: Federal Contracting Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to federal contracting, focusing on transparency, job impact, and benefits for military personnel.

Document 1: prohibit a small business receiving a guaranteed loan through the Small Business Administration (SBA) from expending more on subcontractors than: (1) 50% of the loan amount received
requires each subcontracting plan submitted to federal agencies to contain assurances that the offeror or bidder will report on subcontracting activities throughout the life of the contract.
Document 2: Requires an executive agency to include the effects on employment within the United States (a jobs impact statement) in the evaluation factors that must be considered in each solicitation for competitive proposals for covered contracts
Allows an offeror's jobs impact statement to include a guarantee that jobs created or retained in the United States will not be moved outside the United States after award of the contract.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the duty treatment of specific goods.

Document 1: revises the article description for certain cigars
duty treatment of cigars certified as handmade
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain parts
suitable for use solely or principally with the apparatus of heading 8535, 8536 or 8537
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty
cerium sulfide pigments

topic.name: Palliative and Paralysis Care Research
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving education, research, and care for individuals with palliative needs and paralysis-related conditions.

Document 1: requires the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award grants for Palliative Care and Hospice Education Centers
expand national research programs in palliative care.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to develop mechanisms to coordinate NIH paralysis research
Authorizes such consortia to conduct basic, translational, and clinical paralysis research.
Document 3: revise the priorities the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) uses in assigning National Health Service Corps (NHSC) members
prioritize assignment of members to areas with a high number of veterans with ailments resulting from traumatic brain injuries.

topic.name: Land Trust and Tribal Recognition
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to land trust issues and the recognition of tribal entities in the United States.

Document 3: Extends federal recognition to the Duwamish Tribe.
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized tribes.

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating campaign contributions and expenditures in federal elections.

Document 1: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971
requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution
Document 2: prohibit the conversion of leadership PAC funds to personal use
defines leadership PAC as a political committee established by a candidate
Document 3: amends the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974
requires the Director of the Congressional Budget Office to determine budget impacts of proposed measures

topic.name: Military Sexual Assault Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving the prevention and response to sexual assault within the military and enhancing women's roles in military operations.

Document 1: Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2014 to revise the sexual assault prevention and response program activities of the Armed Forces.
Requires the Special Victims' Counsel to provide advice to assault victims on the advantages and disadvantages of prosecuting such assaults.
Document 2: This bill requires the Department of State, in coordination with the Department of Defense, to seek to increase the participation of women receiving training under the International Military Education and Training program.

topic.name: Medicare Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming and improving Medicare programs and benefits.

Document 1: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
establish a PAC value-based purchasing program
Document 2: amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
makes certain subsidies available to Medicare prescription drug program beneficiaries
Document 3: amends part C (Medicare+Choice) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
determine and announce annually for 2017 and each subsequent year the annual Medicare Advantage capitation rate

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses laws aimed at protecting consumers in financial transactions, particularly regarding credit, debt, and foreclosure practices.

Document 1: Repaying the American Taxpayer Act of 2009 - Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008
requires payment into the Treasury for reduction of the public debt
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit a creditor from extending credit to a consumer under an open end consumer credit plan
Sets forth criminal penalties for violations of this Act.
Document 3: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor
Permits a Chapter 13 bankruptcy plan to modify the rights of claim holders with respect to a claim for a loan originated before the effective date of this Act

topic.name: Transportation and Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving safety and operational efficiency in transportation sectors including rail and aviation.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to require the Secretary of Labor to conduct health and safety related accident investigations in coal or other mines.
Requires an independent accident investigation by an independent panel appointed by the Secretary of Health and Human Services for any accident involving three or more deaths.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for Amtrak capital and operating grants, including accessibility improvements for individuals with disabilities.
Requires the Federal Railroad Administration to approve a new rail tunnel alignment in Baltimore to increase train speed and service reliability.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Homeland Security for the Transportation Security Administration for FY2010-FY2011.
Directs the Assistant Secretary to establish a risk-based system to verify that all cargo transported on inbound domestic and foreign passenger aircraft is screened for explosives.

topic.name: Federal Assistance Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various federal legislative measures aimed at providing assistance and resources for specific communities and needs, including housing, community service, and nutrition.

Document 1: Amends the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996
providing tribes with block grants for affordable and self-determined housing activities.
Document 2: Amends the National and Community Service Act of 1990
involving program participants in emergency and disaster preparedness, relief, and recovery.
Document 3: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act
require the Secretary of Agriculture to provide performance awards to states with outstanding performance.

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Small Businesses
topic.description: This cluster discusses various tax-related legislation aimed at supporting and incentivizing small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: allows a new business-related tax credit for 25% of equity investments
has gross revenues for the taxable year of less than $1 million
Document 2: replaces the marginal income tax rates with a single rate of 17% on individual taxable income
includes in taxable income the taxable income of each dependent child under the age of 14
Document 3: allows a qualified small business a tax deduction equal to 20% of the lesser of qualified domestic business income or taxable income
defined as any employer engaged in a trade or business if such employer had fewer than 500 full-time employees

topic.name: Tax Incentives and Relief
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at providing tax credits, exemptions, and relief for specific groups, including commercial fishermen, veterans, and estate transfers.

Document 1: tax credit for excessive fuel costs
Document 2: repeal the estate and generation-skipping transfer taxes
maximum 35% gift tax rate and a $5 million lifetime gift tax exemption
Document 3: tax credits for veterans who purchase a franchise

topic.name: Health and Energy Policy
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative measures related to health care, food safety, and energy production and regulation in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Energy to establish a technology-neutral program to evaluate and support projects for the production in the United States of significant quantities of molybdenum-99 for medical uses.
Prohibit the Nuclear Regulatory Commission from issuing a license for the export of highly enriched uranium from the United States for medical isotope production purposes.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem a food to be misbranded if it was manufactured, processed, packed, or held in a facility that is not registered.
Requires the owner, operator, or agent of a food facility to conduct a hazard analysis and implement effective preventive controls.
Document 3: Establishes in the Treasury the Clean Energy Investment Fund, consisting of amounts appropriated for expenses to implement the loan guarantee program to provide incentives for innovative technologies.
Authorizes the Secretary to adjust the amount of, or manner of collection of, fees as necessary to promote eligible projects.

topic.name: Regulatory Amendments and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing regulatory frameworks and oversight in various sectors, including mining safety, investment practices, and corporate governance.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to increase from 5 to 10 the minimum number of years of practical mining experience a person selected by the Secretary of Labor for appointment as a mine inspector must have.

Requires mine inspectors assigned to the inspection and investigation of individual mines to have at least 2 years previous experience in the particular type of mining operation where such inspections are to be made.
Document 2: Establishes the Corporate Responsibility Stock Index Fund as an investment option under the Thrift Savings Fund.

Directs the Federal Retirement Thrift Investment Board to select a minimum of one index for such Fund comprised of stocks that have been analyzed and selected using basic criteria, including corporate governance, environmental practices, workplace relations and benefits.

topic.name: Labor and Animal Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to labor rights and the humane treatment of animals in agricultural and research contexts.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to provide that any enterprise or institution owned and operated by an Indian tribe is not considered an employer.
Provides labor sovereignty for Indian tribes.
Document 2: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to apply standards for humane treatment to farm animals.
Focuses on humane treatment in agricultural research facilities.
Document 3: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act to redefine 'oppressive child labor.'
Addresses child labor prohibitions related to tobacco plants.
